label: Community Rule 1QS (Serekh HaYakhad) — Complete
summary_plain: The Community Rule governs the covenant community at Qumran. Those who enter the covenant commit to seek YHWH with all their heart and soul, to do what is good and upright before him, and to walk no more in the stubbornness of a guilty heart. New members undergo examination and a two-year probationary period. The community practices common meals, common prayer, and nightly Torah study. The Two Spirits doctrine teaches that YHWH created two spirits — the Spirit of Truth and the Spirit of Deceit — and all humanity walks in their ways. The community is governed by the sons of Tzadok and by the council of the community. The rule concludes with a hymn praising YHWH’s righteousness.
summary_scholarly: The Community Rule (1QS — Serekh HaYakhad) was among the first seven scrolls found in Cave 1 in 1947. It is the foundational governance document of the Qumran community. The text includes: entry procedures and oath formulas (Col I-III), the Two Spirits doctrine (Col III:13-IV:26), community discipline and hierarchy (Col V-IX), and a closing hymn (Col X-XI). The Two Spirits doctrine is a dualistic theological framework unlike anything in the Masoretic canon — YHWH created both spirits and appointed them until the final visitation. The community structure presupposes a functioning alternative society with its own legal system, calendar, and liturgy. Multiple copies were found in Cave 4 (4Q255-264, 4Q280, 4Q286-287), showing the text’s central importance.

key_terms.definition: Hebrew for ‘Rule of the Community’ or ‘Order of the Unity.’ The foundational governance document of the Qumran covenant community, prescribing entry procedures, discipline, hierarchy, and communal practices.

1QS Col III-IV teaches that YHWH created two spirits — the Spirit of Truth (Ruakh Ha’Emet) and the Spirit of Deceit (Ruakh Ha’Avel). All humanity is divided between these two spirits. The Angel of Light leads the sons of righteousness; the Angel of Darkness leads the sons of deceit. YHWH will destroy the Spirit of Deceit at the final visitation.
The ‘Sons of Tzadok’ — the priestly line that governed the Qumran community. They are the priests who kept the charge of YHWH’s sanctuary when the sons of Yisra’el strayed.

fill_blank.prompt: To seek YHWH with all the heart and with all the soul, to do what is good and upright before him as he commanded through Moshe and through all his servants the ______.
fill_blank.source_quote: To seek YHWH with all the heart and with all the soul, to do what is good and upright before him as he commanded through Moshe and through all his servants the prophets.

fill_blank.prompt: He created the spirits of Light and Darkness, and upon them he founded every ______.
fill_blank.source_quote: He created the spirits of Light and Darkness, and upon them he founded every deed.

multiple_choice.question: How long is the probationary period for someone entering the community?
multiple_choice.source_quote: If it is his destiny to enter the community, he shall be inscribed among his brethren in the order of his rank. After completing one year he shall be examined, and after completing a second year he shall be admitted to full membership.

multiple_choice.question: According to the Two Spirits doctrine, what did YHWH create?
options: Two spirits — the Spirit of Truth and the Spirit of Deceit
Two torot — one written and one oral
Two calendars — one solar and one lunar
multiple_choice.source_quote: He created the spirits of Light and Darkness, and upon them he founded every deed.

faq.question: What does the Community Rule reveal about covenant community self-governance?
faq.answer: The Community Rule (1QS) demonstrates that the Qumran community operated as a fully self-governing covenant society. They had their own entry procedures, judicial system, economic structure (shared property), liturgical calendar, and disciplinary code. They did not rely on the Jerusalem Temple establishment for authority. Their governance derived directly from the Torah as interpreted by the Teacher of Righteousness and the sons of Tzadok. This is significant for the Great Eraser framework: the community proved that covenant identity can be maintained without institutional dependency. When the Temple was corrupt, they built an alternative. When the priesthood was compromised, they preserved the priestly functions within their own community. The Community Rule is a blueprint for covenant survival outside institutional control — the same challenge every scattered covenant community has faced since.

faq.question: Why does the Two Spirits doctrine matter for understanding YHWH’s sovereignty?
faq.answer: The Two Spirits doctrine (1QS III:13-IV:26) states that YHWH created both the Spirit of Truth and the Spirit of Deceit. This is not dualism in the Zoroastrian sense — it is radical monotheism. YHWH is sovereign over both spirits. The Angel of Darkness operates only within the boundaries YHWH has set, and YHWH will destroy him at the appointed time. The Great Eraser framework benefits from obscuring this doctrine. If the Spirit of Deceit operates by YHWH’s permission and within YHWH’s timeline, then the Great Eraser itself — the systematic suppression of covenant identity — operates within YHWH’s sovereign plan and has an expiration date. The Community Rule teaches that the present age of darkness is temporary. YHWH has appointed the time of its end. This is the theological foundation for the claim that identity erasure will not succeed permanently.
